meta.title: agentsys
meta.description: A modular runtime and orchestration system for AI agents. 19 plugins, 47 agents, 40 skills - structured pipelines for Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI, Cursor, and Kiro.
meta.author: Avi Fenesh

hero.title: A modular runtime and orchestration system for AI agents
hero.subtitle: AI models can write code. That's not the hard part anymore. The hard part is everything around it - task selection, branch management, code review, artifact cleanup, CI, deployment. AgentSys is the runtime that orchestrates agents to handle all of it.

problem.title: The bottleneck isn't code generation
problem.description: Frontier models write good code. That's solved. What's not solved is context management, compaction amnesia, task drift, skipped steps, token waste, babysitting, and repetitive requests. You shouldn't have to orchestrate each phase manually, every single session.
points: Context management - models forget what they're doing mid-session
Compaction amnesia - long sessions get summarized, losing critical state
Task drift - without structure, agents wander from the actual goal
Skipped steps - agents skip reviews, tests, or cleanup when not enforced
Token waste - using LLM calls for work that static analysis can do faster
Babysitting - manually orchestrating each phase of development
Repetitive requests - asking for the same workflow every single session

approach.title: Code does code work. AI does AI work.
approach.description: Detection uses regex, AST analysis, and static analysis - fast, deterministic, no tokens wasted. Judgment uses LLM calls for synthesis, planning, and review - where reasoning matters. Result: 77% fewer tokens for drift-detect vs multi-agent approaches.

commands.name: /next-task
commands.tagline: Task to production, fully automated
commands.description: 12-phase workflow: discovers tasks, explores codebase, plans implementation, writes code, reviews, and ships. One plan approval unlocks autonomous execution through to merged PR.

commands.name: /prepare-delivery
commands.tagline: Pre-ship quality gates
commands.description: Pre-ship quality gates: deslop, simplify, review loop, delivery validation, docs sync

commands.name: /gate-and-ship
commands.tagline: Quality gates then ship
commands.description: Quality gates then ship - chains /prepare-delivery then /ship

commands.name: /agnix
commands.tagline: Lint agent configs before they break
commands.description: 385 validation rules (102 auto-fixable) for Skills, Memory, Hooks, MCP, and Plugins across 10+ AI tools including Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Codex CLI, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Cline, Windsurf, Roo Code, and Amp. SARIF output for GitHub Code Scanning.

commands.name: /ship
commands.tagline: Branch to merged PR in one command
commands.description: Commits, pushes, creates PR, monitors CI, waits for auto-reviewers, addresses every comment, merges, deploys, and cleans up. Platform auto-detection for GitHub Actions, Railway, Vercel, and more.

commands.name: /deslop
commands.tagline: Kill AI slop before it ships
commands.description: 3-phase detection pipeline finds debug statements, placeholder text, verbose comments, TODOs, and over-engineering patterns. Certainty-graded findings across JS/TS, Python, Rust, Go, Java.

commands.name: /perf
commands.tagline: Evidence-backed performance investigation
commands.description: 10-phase methodology with baselines, breaking point analysis, hypothesis generation, profiling, and controlled experiments. Based on recorded real investigation sessions.

commands.name: /drift-detect
commands.tagline: Find what's documented but not built
commands.description: AST-based plan vs code analysis. JavaScript collectors gather data fast, one Opus call does semantic matching. Tested on 1,000+ repositories. 77% token reduction vs multi-agent approaches.

commands.name: /audit-project
commands.tagline: Multi-agent code review that iterates until clean
commands.description: Up to 10 specialized agents (security, performance, architecture, database, API, frontend, backend, DevOps) run based on your project. Iterates until zero open issues remain.

commands.name: /enhance
commands.tagline: Analyze everything that shapes agent behavior
commands.description: Seven parallel analyzers check your prompts, agents, plugins, docs, hooks, and skills. Certainty-graded findings with auto-fix support. Auto-learns false positives over time.

commands.name: /repo-intel
commands.tagline: Unified static analysis for AI agents
commands.description: Git history intelligence, AST symbol mapping, and project metadata in one plugin. 20 query types backed by a Rust binary. 9 plugins consume the data automatically.

commands.name: /sync-docs
commands.tagline: Keep docs in sync with code
commands.description: Finds outdated references, stale examples, missing CHANGELOG entries, and version mismatches. Auto-fixes safe issues like version numbers and CHANGELOG entries.

commands.name: /learn
commands.tagline: Research any topic, build a learning guide
commands.description: Progressive discovery gathers 10-40 online sources, scores them by quality, and synthesizes a structured guide with RAG index. Creates reusable knowledge in agent-knowledge/.

commands.name: /consult
commands.tagline: Get a second opinion from another AI tool
commands.description: Cross-tool AI consultation via ACP (Agent Client Protocol). Ask Gemini, Codex, Claude, OpenCode, Copilot, or Kiro for a second opinion. Supports ACP and CLI transports, session continuations, context injection, and user-extensible provider registry.

commands.name: /debate
commands.tagline: Structured adversarial debate between AI tools
commands.description: Multi-round structured debates between any two AI tools as proposer and challenger. Evidence-backed arguments, mandatory counterpoints, and a verdict from the orchestrator. Supports ACP transport for Kiro participation.
commands.example: /debate claude vs kiro about microservices vs monolith

commands.name: /web-ctl
commands.tagline: Browser automation for AI agents
commands.description: Navigate, authenticate, and interact with web pages via headless Playwright. Persistent encrypted sessions, human-in-the-loop auth handoff with CAPTCHA detection, and anti-bot measures.

commands.name: /release
commands.tagline: Versioned release with ecosystem detection
commands.description: Discovers how your repo releases before executing. Checks for semantic-release, release-it, goreleaser, changesets, cargo-release, then falls back to generic tag-and-publish. Supports 12+ ecosystems.

commands.name: /skillers
commands.tagline: Learn from your workflow patterns
commands.description: Reads Claude Code conversation transcripts, identifies recurring patterns, clusters them into weighted themes, and suggests skills, hooks, or agents to automate them. No per-turn overhead.

commands.name: /onboard
commands.tagline: Oriented in any codebase in 3 minutes
commands.description: Pure JS collector pre-structures project data (68ms median, 74% token savings vs manual), then Opus synthesizes a guided tour. Validated on 100 repos across 8 languages. Supports 12 manifest types and monorepo detection.

commands.name: /can-i-help
commands.tagline: Match contributor skills to project needs
commands.description: Collects 5 contributor signals (good-first areas, test gaps, doc drift, bugspots, open issues), asks about developer background, then recommends specific areas with concrete first steps.

philosophy.title: One agent, one job, done extremely well
philosophy.description: Same principle as good code: single responsibility. The exploration-agent explores. The implementation-agent implements. Specialized agents with narrow scope and clear success criteria.

philosophy.title: Pipeline with gates, not a monolith
philosophy.description: Same principle as DevOps. Each step must pass before the next begins. Can't push before review. Can't merge before CI passes. Hooks enforce this - agents cannot skip phases.

philosophy.title: Tools do tool work, agents do agent work
philosophy.description: If static analysis, regex, or a shell command can do it, don't ask an LLM. Pattern detection uses pre-indexed regex. File discovery uses glob. The LLM only handles what requires judgment.

philosophy.title: Right model for the task
philosophy.description: Opus for complex reasoning and analysis. Sonnet for pattern matching and validation. Haiku for mechanical execution. Quality compounds - early phases deserve the best model.

philosophy.title: Validate plan and results, not every step
philosophy.description: Approve the plan. See the results. The middle is automated. One plan approval unlocks autonomous execution through implementation, review, cleanup, and shipping.

philosophy.title: Composable, not monolithic
philosophy.description: Every command works standalone. /deslop cleans code without /next-task. /ship merges PRs without the full workflow. Pieces compose together, but each piece is useful on its own.

workflow_steps.name: Policy Selection
workflow_steps.description: Choose task source, priority filter, and stopping point

workflow_steps.name: Task Discovery
workflow_steps.description: Shows top 5 prioritized tasks - you pick one

workflow_steps.name: Worktree Setup
workflow_steps.description: Creates isolated branch and working directory

workflow_steps.name: Exploration
workflow_steps.description: Deep codebase analysis to understand context

workflow_steps.name: User Approval
workflow_steps.description: You review and approve the plan (last human interaction)

workflow_steps.name: Pre-Review
workflow_steps.description: Runs deslop-agent and test coverage checker

workflow_steps.name: Delivery Validation
workflow_steps.description: Verifies tests pass, build works, requirements met

workflow_steps.name: Ship
workflow_steps.description: Creates PR, monitors CI, addresses comments, merges

highlights: New /onboard plugin - codebase orientation in 3 minutes, 74% token savings, validated on 100 repos
New /can-i-help plugin - contributor guidance with 5 data-backed signals
New /repo-intel plugin (originally /git-map) - 21 git history queries backed by Rust binary
15 plugin READMEs written or upgraded, agent-analyzer v0.2.0 query tuning
Agent-analyzer Phase 1 - git intelligence with 21 queries
New /debate plugin - structured adversarial debates between AI tools
User-extensible provider registry for 6 AI tools
New /web-ctl plugin - browser automation with encrypted session persistence
Human-in-the-loop auth handoff with CAPTCHA detection
Multi-platform support: Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI

recommended.name: GitHub CLI (gh)
recommended.description: For PR operations and issue discovery

research.knowledge_base: 8,000 lines of curated documentation from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft
research.token_reduction: 77% fewer tokens for drift-detect vs multi-agent approaches
